channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: ECOSOC Consultative Status 2026: Gain Access to UN Decision-Making Platforms
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The ECOSOC Consultative Status 2026 offers non-governmental organizations a unique opportunity to engage directly with global decision-making processes at the United Nations. Designed for NGOs seeking to influence international policy and collaborate at the highest level, this status enables organizations to contribute meaningfully to discussions shaping global development, human rights, and sustainability. About the Program […]

The post ECOSOC Consultative Status 2026: Gain Access to UN Decision-Making Platforms appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The ECOSOC Consultative Status 2026 offers non-governmental organizations a unique opportunity to engage directly with global decision-making processes at the United Nations. Designed for NGOs seeking to influence international policy and collaborate at the highest level, this status enables organizations to contribute meaningfully to discussions shaping global development, human rights, and sustainability.

About the Program

The Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC) is one of the six principal organs of the United Nations, responsible for coordinating work on economic, social, and environmental issues. Through consultative status, ECOSOC creates a formal channel for NGOs to participate in its activities and those of its subsidiary bodies.

This relationship is governed by ECOSOC Resolution 1996/31, which sets out the rights, responsibilities, and eligibility requirements for NGOs. Organizations granted consultative status can attend official UN meetings, submit written and oral statements, organize side events, and engage with Member States and other stakeholders.

Applications submitted by 1 June 2026 will be reviewed by the ECOSOC Committee on NGOs in 2027, with final decisions made by ECOSOC. While the process can take time, it opens the door to long-term engagement with the UN system.

Why This Opportunity Matters

For NGOs working to create impact at local, national, or international levels, gaining ECOSOC consultative status represents a significant step toward global visibility and influence. It provides a formal channel to bring grassroots perspectives into international policy discussions and ensures that civil society voices are represented in shaping global agendas.

At a time when global challenges require collaborative and inclusive solutions, this opportunity strengthens the role of NGOs in bridging the gap between communities and international institutions.

What Participants Will Gain

Organizations granted consultative status benefit from direct access to United Nations platforms and processes. This includes the ability to participate in key meetings such as the High-level Political Forum on Sustainable Development and various ECOSOC commissions.

Beyond access, NGOs gain credibility and recognition as official contributors to global discussions. This can enhance partnerships, improve funding opportunities, and strengthen advocacy efforts. The ability to submit statements and organize side events also allows organizations to highlight their work and influence policy conversations in meaningful ways.

In addition, consultative status facilitates networking with government representatives, international agencies, and other civil society actors, creating opportunities for collaboration and knowledge exchange.

Who Can Apply

This opportunity is open to non-governmental, non-profit organizations at the international, regional, sub-regional, and national levels. Organizations must demonstrate that their work aligns with the mission and priorities of the United Nations.

Applicants should have a clear organizational structure, the ability to represent their members, and transparent governance systems. They must also be independent from government control and operate on a non-profit basis.

Eligible Areas

Organizations from all countries are eligible to apply, provided they meet the requirements outlined by ECOSOC. This makes the opportunity globally accessible to NGOs working across diverse sectors and regions.

Application Process and Deadline

To apply, organizations must complete the official online application form, ensuring that all required information and supporting documents are submitted. The application includes detailed questions about the organization’s structure, activities, and alignment with UN priorities.

After submission, applications undergo an internal review process, followed by evaluation by the ECOSOC Committee on NGOs. Final decisions are made by ECOSOC based on the Committee’s recommendations.

The deadline to apply is 1 June 2026, and late submissions will not be considered for the 2027 review cycle. Given the complexity of the process, applicants are strongly encouraged to begin preparations early.

Learn more on the official ECOSOC website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post ECOSOC Consultative Status 2026: Gain Access to UN Decision-Making Platforms appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the GrowthAfrica ESO Uplift Programme 2026: Free Capacity Building for ESOs in Africa
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Entrepreneur Support Organisations (ESOs) across Africa now have an exciting opportunity to strengthen their impact through the ESO Uplift Programme 2026, an action-oriented capacity-building initiative by GrowthAfrica. Designed to support incubators, accelerators, innovation hubs, and business support programmes, this fully funded programme helps organisations improve their ability to support and scale social impact enterprises (SIEs) […]

The post Apply for the GrowthAfrica ESO Uplift Programme 2026: Free Capacity Building for ESOs in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Entrepreneur Support Organisations (ESOs) across Africa now have an exciting opportunity to strengthen their impact through the ESO Uplift Programme 2026, an action-oriented capacity-building initiative by GrowthAfrica.

Designed to support incubators, accelerators, innovation hubs, and business support programmes, this fully funded programme helps organisations improve their ability to support and scale social impact enterprises (SIEs) across Africa.

If your organisation works with startups tackling social, environmental, or economic challenges, this programme could be the perfect opportunity to grow your impact.

About GrowthAfrica

Founded in 2002, GrowthAfrica is one of Africa’s leading Pan-African accelerator and entrepreneur support organisations.

The organisation focuses on helping impactful enterprises grow through:

  • Tailored business acceleration programmes
  • Strategic business guidance
  • Access to investment opportunities
  • Market entry support for international companies
  • Building strong entrepreneur support ecosystems across Africa

GrowthAfrica also works closely with peer ESOs to strengthen the wider entrepreneurial ecosystem across the continent.

About the ESO Uplift Programme

The ESO Uplift Programme is part of the Social and Impact Entrepreneurship (SIE) Project, co-funded by the Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation (SDC).

The programme is implemented by GrowthAfrica in collaboration with:

  • Draper Richards Kaplan Foundation (DRK)
  • Amahoro Coalition

The initiative targets ESOs in:

  • Kenya
  • Uganda
  • Rwanda
  • Zambia
  • Ghana
  • Egypt

Its main goal is to strengthen these organisations so they can better support and scale social impact enterprises.

Unlike traditional training programmes, the ESO Uplift Programme follows a learning-by-doing approach, where participants apply frameworks directly within their organisations.

This means participants will:

  • Carry out practical analyses
  • Develop implementation plans
  • Redesign support programmes
  • Collaborate with peer ESOs across Africa
  • Receive mentorship and advisory support

What You Will Gain

The programme is designed to help build sustainable, high-performing ESOs.

1. Clarity on Your Value Creation

Participants will identify:

  • the right target group
  • ideal geography
  • best programme offerings

This helps organisations focus their energy where it matters most.

2. Stronger Internal Capabilities

Build stronger operations and improve team capacity to deliver consistent results and scale confidently.

3. Resources for Sustainability

Receive practical guidance on:

  • fundraising
  • partnerships
  • revenue generation
  • impact measurement

4. Peer Learning Across Africa

Join a cohort of ESOs across six countries and benefit from shared learning, collaboration, and cross-border insights.

5. Continued Support After the Programme

At the end of the programme, 5 ESOs will be selected to continue receiving dedicated support from GrowthAfrica.

6. No Participation Fee

Thanks to co-funding by the Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation, participation is completely free of charge.

Programme Impact at a Glance

GrowthAfrica has already made significant impact across Africa:

  • 27 team members
  • 7 countries
  • 100+ projects delivered
  • 5,000+ entrepreneurs supported
  • 45,000+ jobs created

Programme Structure

The programme runs for 12 months, beginning in July 2026, and is divided into three practical phases.

Phase 1: Initial Analysis

Time Commitment:

Approximately 4 hours per week

Includes:

  • 4 virtual workshops (2.5 hours each)
  • Individual follow-up and mentoring sessions
  • Pre-reading and practical materials
  • Identification of gaps in your current offerings

This phase helps organisations understand where improvements are needed.

Phase 2: Programme Redesign

Time Commitment:

Approximately 2–3 hours per week

Includes:

  • Planning programme redesign with your team
  • Individual advisory sessions
  • Peer experience-sharing sessions

This stage focuses on redesigning your existing support programmes for stronger impact.

Phase 3: Programme Execution

Time Commitment:

Flexible

Includes:

  • Submission of implementation plans for feedback
  • Launch of your revised programme
  • Monthly check-ins for ongoing support

This final stage ensures real implementation and sustainable change.

Eligibility Criteria

The programme is open to organisations running:

  • incubators
  • accelerators
  • innovation hubs
  • business support programmes

You do not need to identify specifically as an ESO—what matters is the work you do.

To qualify, your organisation must meet the following criteria:

1. Registered and Operating

Your organisation must be legally registered and actively operating in:

  • Kenya
  • Uganda
  • Rwanda
  • Zambia
  • Ghana
  • Egypt

2. You Support Social Impact Enterprises

You must work with businesses that generate revenue while intentionally addressing:

  • social challenges
  • economic challenges
  • environmental challenges

3. At Least 2 Years of Experience

Your organisation should have a minimum of two years of experience supporting startups or post-revenue ventures through:

  • acceleration
  • mentorship
  • training

4. Senior Leadership Commitment

A CEO, Co-founder, or Managing Director must attend all workshops to ensure strategic continuity and decision-making authority.

5. Working Proficiency in English

The entire programme is conducted in English, so participants must have working proficiency.

How to Apply

Interested organisations can APPLY HERE

Early applications are strongly encouraged, especially for organisations looking to strengthen their support systems and scale their impact across Africa.

The GrowthAfrica ESO Uplift Programme 2026 is more than just a training programme—it is a strategic opportunity for Entrepreneur Support Organisations to redesign, strengthen, and future-proof their support systems.

With no participation fee, expert mentorship, peer learning, and the potential for continued post-programme support, this initiative offers exceptional value for ESOs committed to building stronger social impact ecosystems across Africa.

If your organisation is ready to level up its impact, now is the time to apply.

Deadline: 31st May 2026

Discover similar opportunities Here

The post Apply for the GrowthAfrica ESO Uplift Programme 2026: Free Capacity Building for ESOs in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Art4Change Storytelling Fellowship 2026 | Paid Fellowship for Young Creatives in Africa, Europe and the Diaspora
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Art4Change Storytelling Fellowship 2026 is now open for applications, offering young creatives a unique opportunity to develop their storytelling skills while documenting real stories of youth innovation. This six-month fellowship supports individuals from Africa, Europe, and the diaspora who are passionate about media, creativity, and social impact. Designed as a hands-on learning experience, the […]

The post Art4Change Storytelling Fellowship 2026 | Paid Fellowship for Young Creatives in Africa, Europe and the Diaspora appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Art4Change Storytelling Fellowship 2026 is now open for applications, offering young creatives a unique opportunity to develop their storytelling skills while documenting real stories of youth innovation. This six-month fellowship supports individuals from Africa, Europe, and the diaspora who are passionate about media, creativity, and social impact.

Designed as a hands-on learning experience, the fellowship provides training, mentorship, and financial support to help participants produce meaningful content that highlights how young people are driving change in their communities.

About the Program

Organised by the Africa-Europe Innovation Platform (AEIP) and implemented by The African Youth Café, the fellowship runs from June to December 2026. It brings together a diverse cohort of young creatives to explore storytelling across multiple formats, including writing, photography, video, and audio.

Participants will engage in structured online training sessions covering key aspects of digital storytelling, such as content creation, narrative development, and production techniques. Alongside training, fellows will receive ongoing mentorship and editorial support to help refine their work.

A core component of the programme is the creation and publication of stories. Fellows will document real-life examples of youth innovation, capturing how young people are developing and applying ideas in fields such as technology, entrepreneurship, and community development. All content produced during the fellowship will be published on a shared digital platform, increasing visibility and reach.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Young people are driving innovation across regions, yet many of their stories remain underrepresented in mainstream narratives. This fellowship addresses that gap by empowering creatives to document and share these experiences from their own perspectives.

By focusing on storytelling as a tool for visibility and influence, the programme contributes to a broader understanding of how innovation happens on the ground. It also creates a space for cross-regional exchange, allowing participants to learn from different contexts and approaches.

For aspiring storytellers and content creators, this fellowship offers both skill development and the opportunity to contribute to meaningful conversations around youth innovation.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants in the fellowship will gain practical experience in digital storytelling through a structured and supportive environment. They will develop technical skills in content creation while also learning how to craft compelling narratives.

Fellows will benefit from mentorship provided by experienced practitioners, helping them improve both the quality and impact of their work. The programme also offers a stipend to cover essential production costs, enabling participants to focus on creating content without financial barriers.

In addition, fellows will build a portfolio of published work, including articles, videos, or multimedia content. This portfolio can support future opportunities in media, communications, or creative industries.

The collaborative nature of the fellowship also allows participants to connect with peers from different regions, expanding their professional networks and perspectives.

Who Can Apply

The fellowship is open to young creatives aged 18 to 35 who are based in Africa, Europe, or part of the diaspora. Applicants should have a strong interest in storytelling, media, or digital content creation.

Candidates are expected to be passionate about documenting youth innovation and able to commit to the full six-month duration of the programme. Prior professional experience is not required, but applicants should demonstrate creativity, motivation, and a willingness to learn.

Eligible Areas

Applicants must be based in Africa, Europe, or within the diaspora. The programme is conducted online, making it accessible across these regions.

Application Process and Deadline

To apply, candidates must complete the online application form and submit basic personal information, relevant experience, and a short proposal outlining the story they wish to develop during the fellowship.

The application deadline is 29 May 2026. Selected fellows will begin the programme in June 2026.

This is an excellent opportunity for young creatives looking to build their skills, gain mentorship, and contribute to documenting impactful stories of innovation.

Learn more and apply on the official AEIP platform.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Art4Change Storytelling Fellowship 2026 | Paid Fellowship for Young Creatives in Africa, Europe and the Diaspora appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: From Classroom to Global Frontlines: Join the 2026 FAO & IVSA Webinar on One Health, Veterinary Leadership, and Global Disease Response
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The global health landscape is evolving rapidly, and veterinary professionals are playing an increasingly critical role at the intersection of animal, human, and environmental health. The upcoming webinar titled “From Classroom to Global Frontlines: Future Vets Driving Global Impact through Digital Learning and One Health” presents a unique opportunity for veterinary students and emerging professionals […]

The post From Classroom to Global Frontlines: Join the 2026 FAO & IVSA Webinar on One Health, Veterinary Leadership, and Global Disease Response appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The global health landscape is evolving rapidly, and veterinary professionals are playing an increasingly critical role at the intersection of animal, human, and environmental health. The upcoming webinar titled “From Classroom to Global Frontlines: Future Vets Driving Global Impact through Digital Learning and One Health” presents a unique opportunity for veterinary students and emerging professionals to gain valuable insights into global animal health systems and leadership pathways.

Organized by the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations Regional Office for Asia and the Pacific and the International Veterinary Students’ Association Asia & Pacific, with support from IVSA SCOH and SCOVE, this webinar is designed to bridge the gap between academic learning and real-world global health practice.

Event Overview

This high-impact virtual session will bring together global institutions and aspiring veterinary leaders to explore the role of veterinarians in addressing some of the world’s most pressing health challenges.

Key Event Details:

  • Date: Friday, 15 May 2026
  • Time: 15:00 (UTC +8)
  • Duration: 125 minutes (approximately 2 hours)
  • Format: Online Webinar
  • Access: Open for public registration
  • Certification: Certificates awarded to all participants

Why This Webinar Matters

The veterinary profession is no longer confined to clinics and farms. Today, veterinarians are central to global health security, contributing to:

  • Disease surveillance and prevention
  • Food safety systems
  • Environmental sustainability
  • Pandemic preparedness and response

This webinar introduces participants to the One Health approach, a globally recognized framework that integrates human, animal, and environmental health systems to address complex health challenges.

Understanding One Health and Its Global Relevance

The One Health concept emphasizes collaboration across disciplines to tackle health threats such as:

  • Transboundary Animal Diseases (TADs)
  • Emerging Infectious Diseases (EIDs)
  • Zoonotic disease outbreaks

Through this session, participants will gain a deeper understanding of how veterinary professionals contribute to:

  1. Global disease control strategies
  2. Public health protection
  3. International policy and governance frameworks

What You Will Learn

This webinar is structured to provide both theoretical insights and practical knowledge. Participants will explore:

1. Global Animal Health Governance

  • Roles and mandates of global organizations such as FAO and the World Organisation for Animal Health
  • International frameworks for disease monitoring and response
  • Policy coordination between countries and institutions

2. Transboundary and Emerging Diseases

  • Understanding how diseases spread across borders
  • Mechanisms for early detection and rapid response
  • Case studies on global disease outbreaks

3. The Role of Veterinarians in One Health Systems

  • Contribution of veterinarians to interdisciplinary health systems
  • Collaboration with public health and environmental sectors
  • Leadership opportunities in global health initiatives

4. Digital Learning and Capacity Development

One of the highlights of this webinar is the introduction to digital learning platforms developed by FAO and WOAH.

Participants will learn how to:

  • Access technical training resources online
  • Engage in peer-to-peer knowledge sharing
  • Continuously develop professional skills through digital tools

Key Benefits of Attending

Participants in this webinar will gain access to a range of valuable benefits, including:

  • Exposure to global veterinary and public health systems
  • Direct insights from international organizations
  • Understanding of career pathways in global health and development
  • Access to digital learning tools and platforms
  • Certificate of participation for professional development

Who Should Attend

This webinar is designed to be inclusive and accessible to a wide audience. It is particularly beneficial for:

  • Veterinary students
  • Early-career veterinary professionals
  • Public health and animal health enthusiasts
  • Individuals interested in global health systems
  • Students seeking international exposure and career guidance

Organizing and Supporting Institutions

The webinar is delivered through a strong collaboration between leading global and student-led organizations:

  • Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations Regional Office for Asia and the Pacific
  • International Veterinary Students’ Association Asia & Pacific
  • IVSA Standing Committee on One Health (SCOH)
  • IVSA Standing Committee on Veterinary Education (SCOVE)

These institutions are at the forefront of advancing veterinary education, global collaboration, and capacity building.

How to Register

Registration for the webinar is open to the public and conducted via an online form.

Steps to Register:

  1. Access the Official Registration Form
  2. Provide your email and required details
  3. Submit your registration before the event date
  4. Join the session on the scheduled date and time

Note: Participants will receive certificates upon completion.

Important Notes

  • The webinar is free and open to all
  • Participants should ensure they join on time to maximize learning
  • A stable internet connection is recommended for the best experience

Conclusion

The “From Classroom to Global Frontlines” webinar is more than just an online event—it is a gateway to understanding the evolving role of veterinary professionals in global health systems.

By connecting students with international organizations, digital learning tools, and real-world applications of veterinary science, this initiative empowers the next generation of veterinarians to lead in a complex and interconnected world.

If you are passionate about animal health, global development, and making a meaningful impact, this webinar is an opportunity you should not miss.

Click Here to learn more about the FAO & IVSA Webinar.

To discover more global Conference opportunities on OFY, visit HERE!

The post From Classroom to Global Frontlines: Join the 2026 FAO & IVSA Webinar on One Health, Veterinary Leadership, and Global Disease Response appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Next Wave Fund 2026: Get $10,000 Funding + Google Training to Launch Your Tech Startup on Kickstarter
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
If you’re an entrepreneur with a bold tech idea, the Next Wave Fund could be the breakthrough opportunity you’ve been waiting for. Backed by Google and Kickstarter, this initiative is designed to support early-stage startups and small businesses building innovative solutions in technology, gaming, and connected products. This program goes far beyond funding—it provides expert […]

The post Next Wave Fund 2026: Get $10,000 Funding + Google Training to Launch Your Tech Startup on Kickstarter appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you’re an entrepreneur with a bold tech idea, the Next Wave Fund could be the breakthrough opportunity you’ve been waiting for. Backed by Google and Kickstarter, this initiative is designed to support early-stage startups and small businesses building innovative solutions in technology, gaming, and connected products.

This program goes far beyond funding—it provides expert mentorship, training, visibility, and access to a global entrepreneurial ecosystem to help you successfully launch and scale your idea.

 

What Is the Next Wave Fund?

The Next Wave Fund is a strategic support program aimed at empowering early-stage technology startups and small businesses. It focuses on projects that push the boundaries of innovation across:

  • Hardware products
  • Software solutions
  • Digital and physical gaming
  • Connected and smart technologies
  • Products using new materials or advanced functionality

The goal is simple: help innovators bring their ideas to life and succeed through crowdfunding.

 

What You Will Receive (Full Support Package)

Selected participants benefit from a comprehensive package that combines funding, training, and exposure:

1. $10,000 Kickstarter Pledge

  • Each selected project receives a $10,000 pledge toward their crowdfunding goal.
  • This pledge is unlocked only if the campaign reaches its funding goal.

2. Expert Guidance from Kickstarter

  • Hands-on mentorship from Kickstarter’s Design & Technology team
  • Support includes:
    • Campaign strategy and positioning
    • Launch preparation and execution
    • Community building and engagement

3. Free Google Training & Certifications

  • Access to high-quality training programs from Google experts
  • Includes:
    • Google AI Professional Certificate
    • Workshops from Grow with Google Coaches

4. Access to Entrepreneur Community

  • Join Kickstarter’s exclusive design and technology community forum
  • Network with like-minded innovators and founders

5. Marketing & Promotion Opportunities

  • Featured promotion via Kickstarter:
    • Newsletters
    • Social media channels
  • Increased visibility to reach potential backers

6. Growth Opportunities with Google

  • Opportunity to apply for Google Accelerator programs
  • Designed to help scale your startup as it grows

 

Eligibility Criteria

To apply for the Next Wave Fund, you must meet the following requirements:

  1. Business Size
    • Must be an entrepreneur or small business with fewer than 20 full-time employees
  2. Project Type
    • Must focus on:
      • Technology (hardware, software, innovation) OR
      • Digital or physical gaming
  3. Location Requirement
    • At least one team member must be:
      • A U.S. citizen
      • A U.S. resident
      • Or an O-1 visa holder
  4. Age Requirement
    • The primary applicant must be 18 years or older
  5. Financial Requirement
    • The project’s bank account must be located in the United States
  6. Platform Compliance
    • The project must meet all Kickstarter rules and guidelines

 

Application & Review Process

  • Applications are reviewed jointly by experts from Google and Kickstarter
  • Evaluation is based on:
    • Innovation and originality
    • Feasibility of the idea
    • Market potential
  • Applicants are notified within six weeks of submission

 

Important Timeline

  • Application Deadline: Rolling basis (applications accepted throughout the year)
  • Project Launch Requirement: Within 6 months of selection
  • Webinar Date: June 2 (optional but highly recommended)

 

Webinar Opportunity

Interested in improving your chances of success?

Join the official webinar hosted by Kickstarter’s Design & Technology team on June 2, where you’ll learn:

  • How to build a winning crowdfunding campaign
  • Key strategies for attracting backers
  • Common mistakes to avoid

If you can’t attend live, you can still register to receive the recording.

 

Frequently Asked Questions (Key Highlights)

Can I apply if I’ve failed a Kickstarter campaign before?

Yes, previous unsuccessful campaigns do not disqualify you.

Can I apply if my campaign is already live?

Yes, ongoing campaigns are eligible.

Do I need to be a first-time entrepreneur?

No, applicants of all experience levels are welcome.

When do I receive the $10,000 funding?

  • Only after:
    • Launching your campaign
    • Successfully reaching your funding goal
  • Funds are released 2–3 weeks after campaign completion

What if my campaign doesn’t reach its goal?

  • Kickstarter uses an all-or-nothing model
  • You will not receive funding unless the goal is met

 

Additional Resources Available

Participants also gain access to:

  • Creator Resource Hub (campaign planning and execution tools)
  • Kickstarter Learning Lab (expert video tutorials)
  • Partner network (marketing, manufacturing, fulfillment support)

 

Final Thoughts

The Next Wave Fund is more than just a financial boost—it’s a launchpad for innovation. With the combined strength of Google’s expertise and Kickstarter’s global platform, this opportunity can help transform your idea into a successful product with real-world impact.

If you’re building the future of technology or gaming, this is your chance to gain funding, mentorship, and global exposure—all in one program.

Apply here

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity,  Click here
  • More global Grant opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Next Wave Fund 2026: Get $10,000 Funding + Google Training to Launch Your Tech Startup on Kickstarter appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Early Career Scholar Programme 2026 at University of Cambridge: A Prestigious Opportunity for Emerging Researchers in Politics and International Studies
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The University of Cambridge, one of the world’s most renowned academic institutions, has announced an exceptional opportunity for early career researchers through its Early Career Scholar (ECS) Programme 2026. Hosted by the Department of Politics and International Studies, University of Cambridge, this initiative is designed to nurture the next generation of scholars in political science […]

The post Early Career Scholar Programme 2026 at University of Cambridge: A Prestigious Opportunity for Emerging Researchers in Politics and International Studies appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Cambridge, one of the world’s most renowned academic institutions, has announced an exceptional opportunity for early career researchers through its Early Career Scholar (ECS) Programme 2026. Hosted by the Department of Politics and International Studies, University of Cambridge, this initiative is designed to nurture the next generation of scholars in political science and international studies.

This 12-month fixed-term programme, beginning in September 2026, offers a unique combination of research development, teaching experience, and professional mentoring. It is specifically tailored to individuals who have recently completed their PhD and are seeking to strengthen their academic careers within a globally respected institution.

About the Early Career Scholar Programme

The Early Career Scholar Programme is structured to provide a comprehensive academic development experience. Successful candidates will engage in both research and teaching, while benefiting from a personalized training and mentoring framework.

Each scholar will receive:

  • A custom-designed learning programme tailored to their research and career goals

  • Dedicated academic mentorship from experienced faculty members

  • Opportunities to contribute to teaching, supervision, and examination at undergraduate and Master’s levels

  • Access to research funding for conferences and academic activities

The programme reflects the department’s commitment to fostering academic excellence, diversity, and interdisciplinary collaboration.

Key Research Areas

Applicants must demonstrate a strong academic background and research interest in one of the following fields:

  1. Middle East Politics

  2. Comparative Politics

  3. International Relations

These areas represent the core strengths of the department, which is widely recognized for its pluralistic and interdisciplinary approach to political studies.

Roles and Responsibilities

Participants in the programme will take on a dynamic academic role that includes:

  • Building and strengthening their research portfolio

  • Delivering and contributing to teaching and curriculum development

  • Supervising undergraduate and postgraduate students

  • Participating in departmental academic activities

  • Supporting examination processes

This combination ensures scholars gain holistic academic experience, preparing them for future roles in academia or research institutions.

Salary and Benefits

The programme offers a competitive salary package:

  • Salary Range: £35,608 – £46,049 per annum

Additional benefits include:

  • Research funding for conference participation and academic development

  • Access to Cambridge’s world-class academic resources

  • Structured mentoring and career guidance

  • Opportunities to collaborate with leading scholars in the field

Eligibility Criteria

To be considered for the programme, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Must be an early career researcher

  • Must have been awarded a PhD no more than one year prior to September 2026

  • Applicants who have not yet received their PhD must:

    • Have submitted their thesis

    • Provide confirmation of a scheduled viva (examination)

Candidates should also demonstrate:

  • A strong academic track record

  • Clear research potential

  • Alignment with the department’s teaching and research profile

Application Requirements

Applicants are required to submit the following documents:

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)

  • Cover Letter, detailing:

    • Interest in the programme

    • Relevant skills and experience

    • How they will contribute to the department

Application Process

To apply:

  1. Register on the Cambridge recruitment system

  2. Complete the online application form

  3. Upload required documents

  4. Quote reference UE49539 in your application

Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interviews in June 2026, which may be conducted either online or in person.

Important Dates

  • Application Deadline: 14 May 2026 (midnight GMT)

  • Interview Period: June 2026

  • Programme Start Date: September 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute issues.

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

The University of Cambridge is committed to fostering an inclusive academic environment. Applications are welcomed from individuals of all backgrounds, and the university actively promotes equality, diversity, and inclusion across its academic community.

Please note that all successful candidates must have the legal right to live and work in the United Kingdom.

Why You Should Apply

This programme offers a rare opportunity to:

  • Launch your academic career at a globally prestigious university

  • Gain valuable teaching and supervision experience

  • Expand your research network and academic visibility

  • Receive mentorship from leading scholars

  • Access funding and institutional support for your research

For early career researchers aiming to make a significant impact in political science or international studies, this programme represents a powerful stepping stone.

Click here for more information and to apply.

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY here

The post Early Career Scholar Programme 2026 at University of Cambridge: A Prestigious Opportunity for Emerging Researchers in Politics and International Studies appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Policy & Project Officer Job at the European University Association (EUA) in Brussels: A Complete Guide to This Prestigious Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
If you are passionate about shaping the future of higher education in Europe and want to work at the intersection of policy, funding, and governance, the Policy & Project Officer position at the European University Association (EUA) offers an exceptional career opportunity. This role is ideal for professionals seeking to contribute to impactful policy development […]

The post Policy & Project Officer Job at the European University Association (EUA) in Brussels: A Complete Guide to This Prestigious Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are passionate about shaping the future of higher education in Europe and want to work at the intersection of policy, funding, and governance, the Policy & Project Officer position at the European University Association (EUA) offers an exceptional career opportunity.

This role is ideal for professionals seeking to contribute to impactful policy development while working in a multicultural and dynamic European environment. Below is a comprehensive breakdown of everything you need to know about this opportunity—from responsibilities and qualifications to benefits and application procedures.

About the European University Association (EUA)

The European University Association represents universities and national rectors’ conferences across Europe. It plays a crucial role in shaping higher education policies, promoting collaboration, and supporting institutional development.

Working at EUA means being part of a network that:

  • Influences European higher education policy
  • Supports universities in governance and funding strategies
  • Promotes innovation and collaboration across institutions

Overview of the Role

Position Title: Policy & Project Officer
 Unit: Governance, Funding and Public Policy Development Unit (GAF)
 Location: Brussels, Belgium
 Contract Type: Full-time, permanent (38 hours/week)
 Application Deadline: 22 May 2026

This role focuses on supporting EUA’s work in developing sustainable funding models and monitoring regulatory frameworks for universities across Europe.

Key Responsibilities

As a Policy & Project Officer, your work will be diverse and impactful. Your responsibilities will include:

1. Coordination & Stakeholder Engagement

  • Organising physical and virtual meetings with EUA members
  • Managing logistics for consultations and working groups
  • Preparing briefing materials and surveys
  • Documenting discussions through minutes and summaries

2. Project Management

  • Supporting project development and implementation
  • Conducting data collection and analysis
  • Organising events and workshops
  • Handling reporting and financial management tasks

3. Policy Support

  • Monitoring trends in funding and governance
  • Drafting policy papers, reports, and briefings
  • Supporting advocacy and policy development efforts

4. Communication & Representation

  • Liaising with EUA members and partners
  • Representing EUA at meetings and events
  • Presenting findings and insights both online and in-person

5. Additional Contributions

  • Preparing background materials and research documents
  • Supporting emerging tasks within the unit

Required Qualifications and Skills

To be considered for this position, candidates must meet the following criteria:

Educational Background

  • A second-cycle degree (Master’s level) in:
    • Economics
    • Political Science
    • Social Sciences or related fields

Professional Experience

  • Minimum of 2 years of experience in:
    • EU affairs
    • Research
    • Higher education policy

Core Skills

  • Excellent command of English
  • Proficiency in at least one additional European language
  • Strong writing and presentation abilities
  • Experience drafting reports, policy papers, and official documents

Technical Competencies

  • Survey design and implementation
  • Data analysis and visualization
  • Familiarity with data analysis tools/software
  • Strong ICT skills and willingness to learn new tools

Personal Attributes

  • Ability to understand complex funding systems
  • Enthusiasm for working in multicultural environments
  • Strong collaboration and communication skills

What EUA Offers

This position comes with a highly attractive benefits package and a supportive working environment.

Employment Benefits

  • Competitive salary (based on experience)
  • Teleworking compensation
  • Full reimbursement of public transport costs
  • Lunch vouchers
  • Pension scheme
  • Comprehensive health insurance

Work Environment

  • Multicultural and diverse team
  • Exposure to European-level policy making
  • Opportunities to collaborate with universities across Europe

Work Flexibility

  • On-site work: Minimum 3 days per week
  • Remote work: Up to 2 days per week

Location Advantage

The EUA office is located in Brussels’ European Quarter, near the Merode metro station, making it easily accessible.

Why You Should Apply

This role offers more than just employment—it provides a platform to:

  • Influence higher education policy in Europe
  • Build a career in international policy and governance
  • Work with leading universities and policymakers
  • Gain valuable experience in EU-level decision-making

Application Process

Click here for more and to apply 

Required Documents

  1. Updated CV
  2. Concise motivation letter
  3. Salary expectations included in your application
  4. Submission Method
    • Email your application to: recruitment@eua.eu

Important Notes

  • Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis
  • Early application is strongly encouraged
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted

The Policy & Project Officer role at the European University Association is a rare opportunity to engage deeply with European higher education systems while contributing to meaningful policy development.

With its strong benefits, international exposure, and impactful responsibilities, this position is ideal for professionals ready to take the next step in their careers.

For more job opportunities click here

The post Policy & Project Officer Job at the European University Association (EUA) in Brussels: A Complete Guide to This Prestigious Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Digital Energy Challenge 2026 Call for Projects: €827,000 Funding Opportunity for Innovative Energy Startups and SMEs Expanding Across Africa
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Digital Energy Challenge 2026 Call for Projects is one of the most significant global funding opportunities targeting innovative digital solutions in the energy sector. Designed to accelerate energy access, improve utility performance, and drive the clean energy transition across Africa, this program offers grants, expert support, and international visibility to selected applicants. If you […]

The post Digital Energy Challenge 2026 Call for Projects: €827,000 Funding Opportunity for Innovative Energy Startups and SMEs Expanding Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Digital Energy Challenge 2026 Call for Projects is one of the most significant global funding opportunities targeting innovative digital solutions in the energy sector. Designed to accelerate energy access, improve utility performance, and drive the clean energy transition across Africa, this program offers grants, expert support, and international visibility to selected applicants.

If you are a startup, SME, or technology innovator working on cutting-edge energy solutions, this opportunity could be a major breakthrough for scaling your project across African markets.

Why This Opportunity Matters

The Digital Energy Challenge is not just a funding program—it is a strategic platform for scaling impactful energy innovations. Since its launch, it has supported dozens of startups and energy utility projects, helping them transition from pilot stages to real-world deployment.

Africa remains a critical focus due to its energy challenges and opportunities. For example, in countries like Nigeria, millions still lack reliable electricity, making it an ideal environment to test scalable, high-impact solutions.

Categories of the Challenge

Applicants can apply under one or both of the following categories:

1. Tech Accelerator

  • Focus: Scaling innovative digital energy solutions
  • Funding: Up to €150,000 per project
  • Location: Any eligible African country

2. Partnership Challenge

  • Focus: Collaborative projects with energy utilities
  • Funding: Up to €400,000 per project
  • Location: Nigeria (in partnership with Abuja Electricity Distribution Company)

Each category targets real-world implementation and measurable impact.

Key Benefits for Selected Applicants

Successful applicants will gain access to a comprehensive support package that goes beyond funding:

Financial Support

  • Grants covering equipment, software, services, and training costs
  • Up to €400,000 depending on category

Technical and Strategic Support

  • Expert guidance in project management and scaling
  • Participation in a specialized bootcamp

Visibility and Networking

  • Exposure through international communication campaigns
  • Access to the Digital Energy Community
  • Collaboration opportunities with African energy utilities

Thematic Focus Areas

Projects must align with one of the following innovation themes:

  1. Grid Planning and Investment Optimization
  2. Microgrid and Distributed Energy Resource (DER) Management
  3. Data Platforms and Integrated Network Visibility

These themes address pressing challenges in energy infrastructure, digitalization, and system efficiency.

Who Can Apply?

Eligible Applicants (Category of People)

This opportunity is open to:

  • Startups
  • Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs)
  • Technology providers with digital energy solutions

Key Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Have fewer than 250 employees
  • Generate less than €50 million in annual turnover
  • Be independent organizations
  • Demonstrate a strong research and development (R&D) component
  • Have no conflict of interest with project partners

Project Eligibility Requirements

To qualify, projects must:

  • Be digital in nature (software, telecom, cloud-based, or data-driven)
  • Show clear innovation or adaptation to local markets
  • Be beyond the idea stage (must already be piloted)
  • Include a strong R&D component
  • Be implementable within 12 months
  • Align with the challenge’s thematic priorities

Eligible Countries

The challenge is open globally, but projects must be implemented in eligible African countries, including:

  • Kenya
  • Nigeria
  • Ghana
  • South Africa
  • Egypt
  • Morocco
  • Tanzania
  • Uganda
  • Zambia
  • And over 40 other African nations

While applicants can come from anywhere in the world, African-based companies are strongly encouraged and given preference.

How to Apply

To apply for the Digital Energy Challenge 2026:

  1. Download the application pack for your chosen category
  2. Attend or watch the official information webinar
  3. Complete the application form and prepare required documents
  4. Submit your application before the deadline

Applicants applying for both categories must submit separate applications for each.

Apply here

Strategic Insights for Applicants

To stand out in this highly competitive challenge:

  • Focus on scalability and real-world impact
  • Demonstrate clear business viability and sustainability
  • Highlight data-driven innovation and digital integration
  • Show alignment with African energy challenges
  • Provide evidence of successful pilot implementation

Projects that combine innovation with practical deployment potential are more likely to succeed.

Deadline

17th June 2026

The Digital Energy Challenge 2026 is more than just a grant—it is a gateway to transforming innovative ideas into scalable solutions that address one of the most pressing global challenges: energy access and sustainability. With substantial funding, expert mentorship, and access to a powerful network, this opportunity is ideal for startups and SMEs ready to scale their impact across Africa. If you are building the future of energy through digital innovation, this is your chance to secure funding, gain global exposure, and make a lasting impact.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Digital Energy Challenge 2026 Call for Projects: €827,000 Funding Opportunity for Innovative Energy Startups and SMEs Expanding Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Barbara Huber Scholarship Program 2026 in Germany: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for Global Legal Scholars
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
If you are an ambitious academic seeking a prestigious international research opportunity in Europe, the Barbara Huber Scholarship Program 2026 offered by the Max Planck Institute for the Study of Crime, Security and Law stands out as one of the most competitive and intellectually rewarding opportunities in the field of law and criminology. This scholarship […]

The post Barbara Huber Scholarship Program 2026 in Germany: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for Global Legal Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are an ambitious academic seeking a prestigious international research opportunity in Europe, the Barbara Huber Scholarship Program 2026 offered by the Max Planck Institute for the Study of Crime, Security and Law stands out as one of the most competitive and intellectually rewarding opportunities in the field of law and criminology. This scholarship is designed to attract exceptional researchers from across the globe and support groundbreaking research that advances knowledge in critical legal disciplines.

About the Barbara Huber Scholarship Program

The Barbara Huber Scholarship Program is a prestigious research initiative established to honor the legacy of Dr. Barbara Huber, a renowned legal scholar known for her contributions to comparative criminal law and international legal research.

The program is hosted by one of the world’s leading research institutions in legal studies, providing scholars with access to world-class academic resources, networks, and intellectual environments.

This scholarship is specifically designed to support innovative research projects that contribute significantly to scientific progress through new perspectives, methodologies, and findings in law-related disciplines.

Fields of Study Covered

Applicants must propose research in one of the following specialized areas:

  • Criminology
  • Public Law
  • Criminal Law

These fields are central to the institute’s research priorities and must align with the applicant’s proposed project.

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Applicants)

The Barbara Huber Scholarship is highly competitive and targets a specific category of applicants:

Eligible Category of People

  • Doctoral candidates (PhD students)
  • Postdoctoral researchers
  • Professors and experienced academics
  • Researchers affiliated with recognized institutions

Key Eligibility Criteria

  • Must be outstanding academics from foreign research institutions
  • Must present a highly innovative research project
  • Research must demonstrate significant potential impact on scientific knowledge
  • Must be working in one of the eligible legal fields

Eligible Countries and Regions

  • Open to applicants worldwide
  • Specifically targeted at international (non-German) researchers from foreign institutions

This makes it an excellent opportunity for applicants from:

  • Africa
  • Asia
  • Europe (outside Germany)
  • North America
  • South America
  • Oceania

Scholarship Benefits and Financial Coverage

The Barbara Huber Scholarship provides generous financial support tailored to the applicant’s academic level:

Monthly Stipend

  • Doctoral candidates: €1,650 per month
  • Postdoctoral researchers: €2,700 per month
  • Professors / senior researchers: €3,200 per month

Additional Benefits

  • Additional funding for:
    • Academic events
    • Research-related travel within Germany or Europe
  • Access to:
    • Advanced research infrastructure
    • Academic networks and collaborations
  • Opportunity to work in a globally respected research environment

Health Insurance Requirement

Applicants must have valid health insurance and provide proof before commencing the program.

Duration of the Scholarship

  • Minimum: 2 months
  • Maximum: 6 months

This flexible duration allows researchers to tailor their stay based on project needs.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must prepare a comprehensive and competitive application package, including:

  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  • List of academic publications
  • Up to three academic publications (in English or German, PDF format)
  • Detailed research proposal (5–10 pages)

Your research proposal is the most critical component and must clearly demonstrate originality, feasibility, and academic relevance.

Apply here

Selection Criteria

The selection process is rigorous and based primarily on:

  • Quality and originality of the research proposal
  • Academic excellence and publication record
  • Relevance of the project to the institute’s research areas
  • Potential contribution to scientific advancement

Only one scholarship per department per year is awarded, making this a highly competitive opportunity.

Why You Should Apply

The Barbara Huber Scholarship offers more than just funding—it provides:

  • Access to one of the world’s leading legal research institutions
  • Collaboration opportunities with top international scholars
  • Exposure to European legal systems and methodologies
  • A strong boost to your academic and professional profile

Strategic Tips for a Successful Application

To maximize your chances of selection:

  1. Develop a highly original research idea
  2. Align your proposal with criminology, public law, or criminal law
  3. Demonstrate clear academic impact and innovation
  4. Highlight previous publications and research achievements
  5. Ensure your proposal is well-structured and evidence-based

Deadline

31st May 2026

The Barbara Huber Scholarship Program 2026 is a rare and prestigious opportunity for legal scholars worldwide to engage in transformative research in Germany. With generous funding, global recognition, and access to elite academic resources, this scholarship is ideal for researchers aiming to make a meaningful contribution to legal science.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Barbara Huber Scholarship Program 2026 in Germany: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for Global Legal Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Become the Next Artist-in-Residence at Third Wave Fund 2026: Fully Paid Global Art and Activism Opportunity for Changemakers
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
If you are an artist passionate about social justice, community transformation, and using creativity as a tool for activism, the Artist-in-Residence opportunity by Third Wave Fund could be a defining moment in your career. This prestigious and impactful program is designed to support artists who are deeply engaged in movements for gender justice and collective […]

The post Become the Next Artist-in-Residence at Third Wave Fund 2026: Fully Paid Global Art and Activism Opportunity for Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If you are an artist passionate about social justice, community transformation, and using creativity as a tool for activism, the Artist-in-Residence opportunity by Third Wave Fund could be a defining moment in your career. This prestigious and impactful program is designed to support artists who are deeply engaged in movements for gender justice and collective liberation.

About the Third Wave Fund Artist-in-Residence Program

The Third Wave Fund Artist-in-Residence program is a unique initiative that merges art, activism, and social justice. It is designed to empower artists who are committed to creating transformative work that advances gender justice movements.

The program is not just about artistic production—it is about collaboration, storytelling, and mobilizing communities through art. Selected artists work closely with the organization to create compelling visual and narrative content that supports advocacy and grassroots organizing.

According to program insights, this residency allows artists to:

  • Develop creative work rooted in activism
  • Collaborate with a movement-driven organization
  • Engage communities through storytelling and artistic expression
  • Contribute to real-world social change

The residency emphasizes that art is one of the most powerful tools for inspiring action and shifting public consciousness.

Benefits of the Residency

Participants selected for this program receive a wide range of benefits, including:

1. Financial Support

The residency is fully paid, ensuring artists can focus entirely on their creative and activist work without financial stress.

2. Collaborative Work Experience

Artists collaborate directly with the Third Wave Fund team, gaining real-world experience in:

  • Movement-building
  • Advocacy campaigns
  • Community engagement

3. Platform and Visibility

Artists gain exposure through:

  • Organizational campaigns
  • Public storytelling platforms
  • Digital and community outreach initiatives

4. Creative Freedom with Purpose

The residency supports artists in creating work that:

  • Reflects lived experiences
  • Challenges systems of oppression
  • Inspires collective action

5. Professional Growth

Participants expand their:

  • Artistic portfolio
  • Network within activism and arts communities
  • Understanding of social justice frameworks

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Categories of People)

This opportunity is specifically designed for:

Primary Target Group

  • Artists engaged in social justice, activism, and community work

Focus Communities

The program prioritizes individuals who are:

  • Queer, trans, and nonbinary people
  • Black, Indigenous, and people of color (BIPOC)
  • Individuals from marginalized or underrepresented communities

The residency emphasizes a “by-and-for” approach, meaning it centers artists who are part of the communities they advocate for.

Eligible Countries and Regions

While the Third Wave Fund is based in the United States, the opportunity is inclusive and globally relevant.

Applicants Can Typically Come From:

  • United States
  • Global South countries
  • Africa, Asia, Latin America
  • Diaspora communities worldwide

However, applicants should demonstrate alignment with gender justice movements connected to the organization’s mission, which is largely U.S.-focused but globally conscious.

Required Qualifications

To qualify for this residency, applicants are expected to demonstrate:

1. Artistic Practice – Proven experience in any artistic discipline (visual arts, digital art, storytelling, multimedia, etc.)

2. Commitment to Social Justice – Strong alignment with gender justice, liberation, and activism

3. Community Engagement – Experience working with or representing marginalized communities

4. Creative Vision – Ability to produce impactful work that inspires change

5. Collaborative Skills – Willingness to work closely with an organization and community stakeholders

What Will You Do During the Residency?

Selected artists will:

  • Create art-driven campaigns for social change
  • Collaborate with activists and organizers
  • Develop storytelling content for advocacy
  • Produce work that amplifies gender justice movements
  • Participate in community-centered creative processes

This is not a passive residency—it is an active, movement-driven experience.

Why This Opportunity Stands Out Globally

The Third Wave Fund residency is different from traditional art programs because:

1. It Merges Art and Activism – Unlike conventional residencies, this program directly connects art to real-world impact.

2. It Centers Marginalized Voices – The program prioritizes artists who are often excluded from mainstream opportunities.

3. It Is Fully Paid – Many residencies require funding from participants, but this one provides financial support.

4. It Builds Movement Power – The goal is not just artistic output but collective liberation and systemic change.

Application Process

Although full details are provided on the official page, applicants should generally prepare:

  • Portfolio of previous work
  • Statement of interest or creative proposal
  • Description of how their work aligns with gender justice
  • Background information and experience

Applicants are encouraged to demonstrate both artistic excellence and social impact.

Apply here

Deadline

13th May 2026

The Artist-in-Residence program by Third Wave Fund is more than just an artistic opportunity—it is a platform for transformation. It empowers artists to use their creativity as a force for justice, equity, and liberation. If you are an artist who believes in the power of storytelling, visual expression, and community engagement to change the world, this residency offers a rare chance to turn your art into action.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Become the Next Artist-in-Residence at Third Wave Fund 2026: Fully Paid Global Art and Activism Opportunity for Changemakers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
